title: Dar Keyis
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=New Republic |image=[[File:Dar_keyis.jpg]] |name=Dar Keyis |homeworld=[[Churba]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 21|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 21]] |birth= |death=[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 24|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 24]] (39:12 [[Great ReSynchronization|GrS]]),''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' establishes a 12-[[Standard month/Legends|month]] [[Standard year/Legends|year]], replacing the old 10-month year format established by [[West End Games]]. ''The Essential Atlas'' notes that the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] begin on 39:3:3. ''[[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 3]]'' establishes that the events of ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: In the Empire's Service]]'' occur nine months after Endor, placing the date of the battle at 39:12 [[Great ReSynchronization|GrS]]. [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Blond]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[New Republic Starfighter Corps/Legends|New Republic Starfighter Corps]] ***[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I didn't think you'd think we were that much fun to hang around with, Lieutenant.''"
"''I don't, but your dumb remarks will start a fight with some locals and ''that'' will be fun.|Dar Keyis and Plourr Ilo|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 23|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 23]]}} '''Dar Keyis''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Churba]] who served as a member of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic Starfighter Corps/Legends|Starfighter Corps]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], nine [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], he was transferred to [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] just prior to the [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Brentaal IV]] and was assigned the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] [[Rogue Ten/Legends|Rogue Ten]]. The New Republic had targeted the planet [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]] as part of an ongoing operation to establish a foothold in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], and to eventually [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|liberate]] the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Keyis participated in the [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)#Assault on the Brentaal IV moonbase|assault]] on the [[Brentaal Moonbase]], helping to secure it as a staging area for a direct assault on the planet itself. He then took part in the [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)#Establishing a beachhead|attack]] on the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Vuultin]], a diversionary feint that allowed the New Republic to establish a base in the city of [[Oradin]]. During the [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)#Evacuation efforts|evacuation of Brentaal IV]]—an effort by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] authorities to spirit away the wealthy and elite citizens of the planet—Keyis' [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]] was destroyed by weapons fire from a [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptor]], and he was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the resulting explosion. ==Biography== ===Early service=== {{Quote|Sorry to have kept you waiting, our debriefing ran a bit long.''"
"''No problem, Captain.|Captain Wedge Antilles and Dar Keyis|}} [[File:Recruits.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Rogue Squadron receives new recruits ahead of the Battle of Brentaal IV.]] Dar Keyis hailed from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Churba]] and was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in service to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic Starfighter Corps/Legends|Starfighter Corps]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], approximately nine [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], Keyis was transferred to [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. He had already participated in eight [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat missions]] before being reassigned to the elite unit, which at the [[Time/Legends|time]] was stationed at the secret [[Base Delta-Nine]]. Keyis' assignment to the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]]—along with the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Standro Jcir]], fellow [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Avan Beruss]], and the [[Tunroth]] [[Xarcce Huwla]]—was part of an effort to bring Rogue Squadron up to full strength prior to the [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Brentaal IV]], a New Republic effort to gain a foothold in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. The new recruits met their [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and [[Executive officer/Legends|executive officer]] [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]]. Assigned the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] of [[Rogue Ten/Legends|Rogue Ten]]Based on the assumption that all [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] members retained their [[Callsign/Legends|call signs]] throughout their service to [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. When [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]] joined Rogue Squadron, he acquired the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] [[Rogue Ten/Legends|Rogue Ten]]; the only two [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] lost prior to his joining the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] were Keyis and [[Standro Jcir]]. Jcir was known to be [[Rogue Twelve]], leading to the assumption that Keyis was Rogue Ten. upon joining the unit, Keyis and his [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] mates underwent [[Flight simulator/Legends|holosimulator]] training for an [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)#Assault on the Brentaal IV moonbase|assault]] on the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]]-held [[Brentaal Moonbase]]. Following a recent [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)#New Republic reconnaissance|reconnaissance mission]] undertaken by Rogue Squadron to [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] and the [[New Republic Provisional Council]] decided that it was time to strike the planet as part of their campaign to liberate the Core Worlds and eventually the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Admiral Ackbar was to lead the [[New Republic task force (Brentaal IV)|task force]] assigned to capture Brentaal IV. ===Assault on Brentaal's moonbase=== {{Quote|Isoto's big ships are too far away to interfere. You are clear to engage enemy, Rogue Leader.|Admiral Ackbar|}} [[File:BattleOfBrentaalIV.jpg|thumb|right|The New Republic assaults the Brentaal IV moonbase.]] New Republic forces staged from the planet [[Recopia]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' launching their campaign against the Imperial forces in the [[Brentaal system/Legends|Brentaal system]]. Keyis, along with the rest of Rogue Squadron, participated in the opening phases of the operation by attacking the Imperial [[Brentaal Moonbase|base]] on one of Brentaal IV's [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. Rogue Squadron and [[Aggressor Wing]] led the assault, facing off against a squadron of elite [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]]. With Imperial [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] too far out of position to assist, the New Republic succeeded in landing [[Soldier/Legends|troops]] and capturing the moonbase. Rogue Squadron returned to Base Delta-Nine following the operation. During downtime for the squadron, Keyis played a game of [[Dejarik/Legends|dejarik]] against Xarcce Huwla when Captain Antilles arrived and asked to us the [[Hologame table/Legends|hologame table]]. Keyis deferred to his commander's use of the device, stating he was going to let Huwla win the match anyway. Antilles used the [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] game table to share information with the squadron that the TIE interceptors present defending the moonbase were from the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Group]], an elite unit under the command of [[Baron/Legends|Baron]]-[[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]]. ===Striking at Brentaal IV=== {{Quote|With Isoto's HQ at Vuultin, we will feint at it with Rogue Squadron, while Colonel Salm's wing safeguards our strike at Oradin and the spaceport there.''"
"''Admiral, any word where this Fel's fighters are?''"
"''Our intelligence assets in Vuultin have spotted Fel there, which is why your mission will be to engage them and prevent them from hitting our assault troops.|Admiral Ackbar briefs Rogue Squadron and Aggressor Wing|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 22|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 22]]}} Along with the rest of Rogue Squadron, Keyis was transferred to the Brentaal IV moonbase in preparation for the New Republic's assault on the planet itself. Between patrol missions, he was playing [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] with several other members of the squadron when Baron Fel's presence on Brentaal IV was confirmed by intelligence reports. Keyis listened as members of the squadron recounted their memories of him—Tycho Celchu and [[Derek Klivian/Legends|Derek "Hobbie" Klivian]] had trained under Fel at the [[Imperial Naval Academy/Legends|Prefsbelt Fleet Camp]] and admitted they never managed to shoot him down in the simulators. [[File:Brentaal-181st.jpg|thumb|left|Rogue Squadron face off against elements of the 181st over Vuultin.]] Some time after, Keyis and Rogue Squadron participated in the subsequent [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)#Establishing a beachhead|assault]] on the Brentaal [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Vuultin]]. Rogue Squadron hit the capital of the planet's ruler, Admiral [[Lon Isoto]], as a diversion, allowing Colonel [[Horton Salm/Legends|Horton Salm's]] [[Aggressor Wing]] to attack the city of [[Oradin]] and capture its [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]. Intelligence had placed Fel and his forces in Vuultin but Rogue Squadron found that the opposition at the city was lighter than expected. Fel had correctly surmised that Oradin was the true target for the New Republic's assault and he inflicted heavy casualties on the [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]] of Aggressor Wing. However, Isoto felt threatened by Rogue Squadron's assault, believing they were attacking him directly, and recalled Fel and his unit away from Oradin. With news that Fel was heading for Vuultin, Ackbar ordered Rogue Squadron to head to the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']], one of the capital ships assigned to his task force. Despite taking heavy losses, the New Republic succeeded in capturing Oradin. Rogue Squadron were billeted at Oradin following the capture of the city. Following the battle, Keyis and Standro Jcir were present in the main [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] when [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Isplourrdacartha Estillo|Plourr Ilo]] profusely thanked Rogue Squadron [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] [[Koyi Komad]] for fixing her [[Maneuvering jet/Legends|maneuvering jets]]. When Ilo asked Komad how she could repay her, Keyis and Jcir made several joking remarks about buying her a planet or a moon as a reward, since Ilo was also the [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] of [[Eiattu 6]]. Ilo offered to take the two pilots for drinks, reasoning that their dumb remarks would start a fight that she could enjoy. ===Battle over Vuultin=== {{Quote|Help Keyis, Nine.''"
"''On him, but too late! Sithspawn!|Rogues Eight and Nine|}} [[File:Keyisdeath.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Dar Keyis dies over Brentaal IV.]] Shortly after the capture of Oradin, an Imperial evacuation [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] arrived at Brentaal IV intent on rescuing the wealthier citizens from the New Republic invasion. Fearful that the escapees would take much of the planet's wealth and leave the economy in ruins, the New Republic Provisional Council authorized a [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)#Evacuation efforts|mission]] to stop the population from evacuating. Rogue Squadron supported Aggressor Wing while they disabled fleeing [[Transport/Legends|transports]] with their [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], first at Vuultin, then in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] above the planet. During the [[Space warfare/Legends|space battle]], Keyis' [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] was targeted by a TIE interceptor. [[Rogue Nine/Legends|Rogue Nine]] attempted to assist Keyis but was too late, and his starfighter was hit by weapons fire from the TIE Interceptor. Keyis' X-wing was destroyed, and he was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the resulting explosion. Despite his death, the New Republic was victorious; the remaining Imperial forces withdrew from the [[Brentaal system/Legends|system]], leaving Brentaal and the disabled civilian [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] under New Republic control. ===Legacy=== [[File:BrentaalFuneral-XWRS26.jpg|thumb|left|The funeral for those lost during the Battle of Brentaal IV.]] {{Quote|Standro and Keyis, two more to add to the squadron's roster of the dead; a roster that began before the squadron did. Jek Porkins. Biggs Darklighter. Dack and Zev and everyone else that flew with us on even one mission. Too many to remember, but too many to allow them to be forgotten. Rogues, now and forever, part of a grand tradition all of them would gladly trade for another second of life.|Wedge Antilles remembers the fallen|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 26|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 26]]}} Following the Battle of Brentaal IV, a mass funeral was held for all those who had been lost during the engagement. The ceremony was held on the hangar deck of a [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Star Cruiser/Legends|star cruiser]]. Fifteen caskets—including ones for Keyis and squadron mate Standro Jcir—were launched into [[Realspace/Legends|space]] while Colonel Salm presided over the funeral. During the ceremony, Antilles reflected on Keyis and Jcir joining Rogue Squadron's roster of the dead and that they should never be forgotten. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I was going to let the Tunroth win, anyway.|Dar Keyis|}} Dar Keyis was a light-skinned Human [[Gender/Legends|male]] with [[Color/Legends|blond]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and stubble on his face. He was considered to be a capable pilot, performing well enough to earn a transfer to Rogue Squadron, an elite unit within the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic military]]. Despite being a relative newcomer to the squadron, Keyis was able to integrate himself into the unit's social structure without difficulty, joining in on sabacc games and joking around with Lieutenant Plourr Ilo over her [[Noble/Legends|noble]] status. ==Equipment== Dar Keyis wore a casual [[Clothing/Legends|outfit]], including a beige-[[Color/Legends|colored]] [[Jacket/Legends|jacket]] with dark brown patches at the shoulders, worn over a white undershirt. He also wore a [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] while piloting his starfighter and a [[Flight helmet/Legends|flight helmet]] with the emblem of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] on either side. Keyis flew a T-65 X-wing starfighter while a member of Rogue Squadron. The fighter was destroyed during the Battle of Brentaal IV when Keyis was killed. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Xwing21.jpg|thumb|right|The cover of ''X-Wing Rogue Squadron 21'', the issue that Dar Keyis debuted in.]] Dar Keyis was created by [[Michael A. Stackpole]] as a new pilot joining Rogue Squadron in the four-[[Star Wars comic books|issue]] ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: In the Empire's Service]]'' story arc.''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: In the Empire's Service]]'' He debuted in the [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 21|first issue of the arc]], and was killed in the [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 24|last issue]]. He was subsequently mentioned in the [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 26|first issue]] of the [[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Family Ties|''Family Ties'']] arc, as Wedge Antilles muses that Keyis is one of the latest Rogues to join their roster of the dead. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Dar Keyis}} *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 21|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 21]] {{1st}} *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 22|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 22]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 23|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 23]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 24|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 24]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 26|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 26]] {{Mo}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars Handbook 1: X-Wing Rogue Squadron|''Star Wars Handbook'' 1: ''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'']] {{Po}} *{{FactFile|14|ROG 1-2|X-Wing Rogue Squadron}} {{Po}} {{StartBox}} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Rogue Ten/Legends|Rogue Ten]] |years=[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] |before=Eventually [[Tarrin Datch/Legends|Tarrin Datch]][[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]] with [[Hosh Hune]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' |before-years=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]][[Tarrin Datch/Legends|Tarrin Datch]] and [[Hosh Hune]] operated as [[Rogue Ten/Legends|Rogue Ten]] during the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. The Battle of Hoth is established to have occurred in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''. |after=[[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]] |after-years=4 ABY}} {{EndBox}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Dar Keyis }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Keyis, Dar}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Rogue Squadron personnel]]
